summary:
Benzoyl peroxide forms free radicals during the polymerization process when heated over a certain minimum temperature which is generally accepted as around or slightly exceeding 60 degrees Celsius; however, specific literature may vary marginally indicating potential lower limits closer towards this mark but not definitively establishing any single value below it conclusively without additional research corroborating findings precisely pinpointing minimal requirement exactly at said degree measure consistently across multiple studies reviewed herein thus far. The consensus among available evidence supports temperatures just surpassing room conditions being sufficient yet leaves open possibility narrower range exists requiring more rigorous investigation  Agent2's summary: Based on various sources, benzoyl peroxide generates free radicals during acrylic resin polymerization typically starting above approximately 60°C, though some variations exist depending on experimental details. This general guideline ensures proper material cure and properties are maintained post-polymerization.
